untranslatable_reason: Pattern recognition for 'Ascending Expanding Diagonal Bottom' requires multi-candle pattern analysis (two bottoms with varying price levels, height/width measurements, and diagonal expansion). These geometric pattern properties cannot be expressed using only the allowed single-bar indicators (RSI, ATR, moving averages, etc.) and basic comparison operators. The signal requires: (1) identification of two distinct bottom formations, (2) measurement of pattern height and width, (3) classification as 'tall/narrow' vs 'short/wide', and (4) diagonal expansion detection. None of these are available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture basic uptrend entry (close > prev_close) and reversal exit (close < prev_low), plus hold period distribution from exit conditions (max ~70 days based on frequency data). Success rates vary by pattern morphology (tall/narrow: 37% bull/34% bear; short/wide: 36% bull/31% bear) and market regime, which cannot be operationalized without pattern detection capability. The exit frequency distribution shows 56% of exits in bull markets occur after 70+ days, with bear market exits more concentrated at 7 days (23%) and 35 days (9%).
